info.title: BryanNet API
info.description:
Backend API for the BryanNet ISP Management Platform.

BryanNet automates the sale, activation, renewal, and monitoring of internet subscriptions. The backend is the single source of business logic for every client -- the admin dashboard, the Telegram bot, and any future customer portal.

### Authentication
Most endpoints require a Bearer JWT obtained from `POST /auth/login`. Pass it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Tokens are tied to a specific admin session record; closing or expiring that session invalidates the token even before its JWT expiry.

A small number of endpoints are intentionally unauthenticated because they are called by the Telegram bot before a customer has any credentials to present (see the Customers and Session sections).

### Error format
All error responses share one envelope:

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Human-readable description of what went wrong."
}
```

Validation errors (422) additionally include an `errors` array with the field-level Pydantic validation details.

### Rate limiting
Selected sensitive or expensive endpoints are rate-limited per client IP (see individual endpoints for limits). Exceeding a limit returns `429 Too Many Requests`.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

429.description: Too many requests -- rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Authenticates an administrator with username and password, creates a new admin session record, logs the login in the audit trail, and returns a JWT bearer token scoped to that session. Rate limited to 5 requests/minute per client IP.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns the identity of the administrator associated with the current bearer token.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns a combined snapshot for the administration screen: headline metrics (admin user count, active sessions, roles, audit/system events today), the most recent audit log entries, currently active sessions, and recent system activity.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Updates the `last_activity` timestamp on an admin session. Used to keep a session alive.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Manually closes a single admin session (logout). Sets `is_active` to false and records a logout reason and time.

patch.summary: Close all sessions for an admin

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Closes every active session belonging to the given admin user, e.g. when forcing a logout everywhere. Returns the number of sessions closed.

schema.title: Response Get All Sessions Api V1 Admin Sessions  Get

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description: Returns admin sessions with optional filters by admin user and active status, sortable and paginated (`page`, `page_size` query params). Note: this endpoint is not itself behind `get_current_admin`, but is intended for authenticated dashboard use.

schema.title: Response Get Active Sessions Api V1 Admin Sessions Active Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns all currently active admin sessions across all administrators.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns a single admin session by its ID.

schema.title: Response Get Admin Sessions Api V1 Admin Sessions Admin  Admin User Id  Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns the full session history for a given admin user.

schema.title: Response Get Active Session Api V1 Admin Sessions Admin  Admin User Id  Active Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns the single currently active session for an admin user, or `null` if none.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Headline numbers for the admin dashboard: customers, active/queued subscriptions, active devices, revenue totals, revenue today, subscriptions expiring today/next 7 days, and new customers today/this month.

schema.title: Response Get Revenue Overview Api V1 Dashboard Revenue Overview Get

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Revenue broken down over a time window. `period` accepts `7d`, `30d`, `month`, or `12m` (default `month`); the response is a list of labeled buckets with revenue totals.

schema.title: Response Get Subscription Breakdown Api V1 Dashboard Subscription Breakdown Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns a count of subscriptions grouped by status (queued, active, expired, cancelled).

schema.title: Response Get Recent Activity Api V1 Dashboard Recent Activity Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns the most recent notable events across the platform (payments, subscriptions, etc.), newest first. `limit` controls how many are returned (default 10).

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Customer with this phone number already exists.

post.description: Creates a customer record directly from the admin dashboard, bypassing the Telegram onboarding flow. Phone number must be unique.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Customer with this phone number already exists.

put.description: Updates a customer's phone number and full name.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Marks a customer account as active.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Marks a customer account as inactive.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

post.description: Called by the Telegram bot the first time a Telegram user interacts with it. Creates a bare customer record keyed by `telegram_user_id` with registration in progress. Not authenticated -- it is the very first touchpoint before any credentials exist.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

patch.description: Sets the customer's full name during the Telegram onboarding flow, identified by `telegram_user_id`. Not authenticated.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

400.description: Customer with this phone number already exists.

patch.description: Sets the customer's phone number during the Telegram onboarding flow, identified by `telegram_user_id`. Phone number must be unique. Not authenticated.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description: Paginated, searchable, sortable list of customers for the admin dashboard. Note: unlike other customer endpoints, this route does not require `get_current_admin` in the current code.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns a single customer by internal ID.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Looks up a customer by phone number.
Looks up a customer by their Telegram user ID. Used by the bot to resume a known customer's session. Not authenticated.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: A plan with this name already exists.

post.description: Creates a new internet plan. Plan names must be unique.

schema.title: Response Get Plans Api V1 Plans  Get

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description: Returns all plans (paginated), including inactive ones. Not authenticated -- used by the bot's plan picker.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: A plan with this name already exists.

put.description: Replaces a plan's commercial details (price, duration, speed, device limits).

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns a single plan by ID.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Marks a plan as active and available for purchase.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Marks a plan as inactive; it stops appearing in active-plan listings but existing subscriptions are unaffected.

schema.title: Response Get Active Plans Api V1 Plans Active Get

get.description: Returns only plans currently marked active -- what a customer should be offered when purchasing. Not authenticated.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

post.description: Creates a subscription for a customer/plan pair. If the customer has no active subscription, the new one starts immediately with status `active`. If the customer already has an active subscription, the new one is created with status `queued` and its start date is set to the current subscription's expiry -- it activates automatically when the current one expires. This is normally called internally by `PaymentService.complete_payment` once a payment is verified, but is also exposed directly for admin-assisted or manual subscription creation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Only queued subscriptions can be cancelled.

patch.description: Cancels a subscription that is still `queued` (not yet active). Active or already-finished subscriptions cannot be cancelled this way.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

429.description: Too many requests -- rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Manually triggers the subscription background job (expiring due subscriptions, activating queued ones whose turn has come, etc.) and returns how many were processed. Rate limited to 10 requests/minute.

schema.title: Response Get Subscriptions Api V1 Subscriptions  Get

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description: Paginated, filterable (customer, plan, status) list of subscriptions for the admin dashboard. Note: unlike other subscription endpoints, this route does not require `get_current_admin` in the current code.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns a single subscription with admin-facing detail (customer name, plan name, remaining days).

schema.title: Response Get Customer Subscriptions Api V1 Subscriptions Customer  Customer Id  Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns every subscription (any status) belonging to a customer.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns the customer's current active subscription, if any.

schema.title: Response Get Queued Subscriptions Api V1 Subscriptions Customer  Customer Id  Queued Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns subscriptions waiting to activate once the current active one expires.
Lightweight status check used by the Telegram bot: whether the customer has an active subscription, the plan name and expiry if so, and how many subscriptions are queued behind it. Not authenticated.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Customer has no active subscription / Device limit reached / Device already exists.

post.description: Registers a new device (MAC address) for a customer. The customer must have an active subscription, and must be under the plan's device limit. Duplicate MAC addresses for the same customer are rejected.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.
Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Deactivates a device without blocking it outright.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Marks a device as approved by the customer (e.g. confirming a new device is theirs).

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Blocks a device from network access. A device that is already blocked cannot be blocked again.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

patch.description: Restores network access to a previously blocked device.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Device does not belong to this customer.

post.description: Swaps one of a customer's devices for another (e.g. new phone). Both devices must belong to the specified customer.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description: Paginated, filterable (search, customer, status) list of devices. Note: unlike other device endpoints, this route does not require `get_current_admin` in the current code.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns a single device by ID.

schema.title: Response Get Customer Devices Api V1 Devices Customer  Customer Id  Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns every device registered to a customer.

schema.title: Response Get Active Devices Api V1 Devices Customer  Customer Id  Active Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns only the customer's currently active devices.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

post.description: Creates a pending payment transaction for a customer/plan pair via the given provider and channel. This does not grant any entitlement by itself -- it is completed separately once the provider confirms it.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description: Paginated, filterable (customer, provider, channel, method, status) list of payments. Note: unlike other payment endpoints, this route does not require `get_current_admin` in the current code.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Only pending payments can be completed.

post.description: Marks a pending payment as successful and, in the same operation, creates the resulting subscription (via `SubscriptionService.create_subscription`) and links it back to the payment. Only pending payments can be completed.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Only pending payments can be cancelled.

patch.description: Cancels a pending payment before it is completed. Only pending payments can be cancelled.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Only successful payments can be refunded.

patch.description: Marks a successful payment as refunded. Only successful payments can be refunded.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

400.description: Only pending payments can be expired.

patch.description: Marks a pending payment as expired, e.g. after a provider callback window has passed. Only pending payments can be expired.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Aggregate payment counts by status (pending, successful, failed, cancelled, refunded, expired) plus total revenue.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns a single payment by its reference.

schema.title: Response Get Customer Payments Api V1 Payments Customer  Customer Id  Get

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

get.description: Returns every payment made by a customer.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

429.description: Too many requests -- rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Runs every background maintenance job (payments, subscriptions, routers, notifications) in one call and logs a single audit entry. Rate limited to 10 requests/minute.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

429.description: Too many requests -- rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Runs payment reconciliation/expiry maintenance. Rate limited to 10 requests/minute.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

429.description: Too many requests -- rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Expires due subscriptions and activates queued ones whose turn has come. Rate limited to 10 requests/minute.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

429.description: Too many requests -- rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Runs router account provisioning/sync/retry maintenance. Rate limited to 10 requests/minute.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token.

429.description: Too many requests -- rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Runs due notification scheduling (reminders, expiry notices, etc.). Rate limited to 10 requests/minute.

422.description: Request body or query parameters failed validation.

429.description: Too many requests -- rate limit exceeded.

get.description: Given a Telegram user ID, returns what the bot should do next: the next onboarding step, a message to show, a keyboard layout, and the customer record if one exists. This is the core state-machine endpoint the Telegram bot polls to decide what screen to show. `first_login` marks a brand-new chat. Not authenticated. Rate limited to 30 requests/minute.
Basic liveness probe. Returns service status and a timestamp. Not authenticated.

tags.name: Administration
tags.description: Aggregated administration overview: metrics, recent audit logs, active sessions, system activity.

tags.description: Admin login session lifecycle: activity tracking, closing sessions, session queries.

tags.description: Customer identity, registration, and the Telegram onboarding flow.

tags.description: Customer devices: registration, approval, blocking, replacement.

tags.name: Automation
